title: olearia lepidophylla
intro: Olearia lepidophylla, also known as the woolly daisy bush, is a shrub native to Western Australia that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Olearia lepidophylla include its potential to treat digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Olearia lepidophylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal benefits, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal remedies.
